name: code-review
description: Portable code-review skill conformance: modes, depth, filing, findings-first anti-thrash (archaeology, Continuity footer, opt-in show ledger, green leftover cleanup), and no-commit behavior

scenarios.name: staged: merge-blockers only
scenarios.prompt:
Review my staged changes in agent-suites/fixtures/sample-app/src/redirect.ts with default merge-blocker filing only. Review only; do not edit files or commit.

Read code-review/SKILL.md first, then inspect the staged diff. Required synthesis header: `Review · staged · Standard · redirect` and **Depth:** Standard.

judge: The review uses default merge-blocker filing and does not file test-inventory or polish findings under Action

scenarios.name: staged: standard structured
scenarios.prompt:
Review my staged changes in agent-suites/fixtures/sample-app/src/redirect.ts before I open a PR. Do not edit files; review only.

Read code-review/SKILL.md first, then inspect the staged diff. Required synthesis header: `Review · staged · Standard · redirect` and **Depth:** Standard.

judge: The transcript contains a structured code review for the requested file without editing files

scenarios.name: commit: standard depth
scenarios.prompt:
Review the latest commit that touches agent-suites/fixtures/sample-app/src/redirect.ts. Default merge-blocker filing. Review only; no edits or commits.

Read code-review/SKILL.md first, then inspect the commit diff. Required synthesis header: `Review · commit · Standard · redirect` and **Depth:** Standard.

judge: The agent reviewed a commit or equivalent diff and produced a Standard-depth synthesis

scenarios.name: pr: thorough default
scenarios.prompt:
Review the PR diff for agent-suites/fixtures/sample-app/src/form.tsx only (main...HEAD). Client-only UI: Thorough depth, do not promote to Full. Default merge-blocker filing. Review only; no edits.

Required synthesis header: `Review · pr · Thorough · sample-form` and **Depth:** Thorough.

judge: The review declares Thorough depth, not Full escalation, for this client-only PR scope

scenarios.name: pr: auth promotes full
scenarios.prompt: Review the PR diff for auth/session handling in agent-suites/fixtures/sample-app/src/auth.ts. Apply depth and escalation rules from the code-review skill. Review only; no edits.

judge: The review escalates to Full depth with an auth/security-related reason and files or clearly discusses merge-blockers

scenarios.name: pr: anti-thrash targeted contextual
scenarios.prompt:
Re-review the PR diff for agent-suites/fixtures/sample-app/src/redirect.ts (main...HEAD) after fixes. Prior Action finding theme_id `query-preservation` is in the ledger (open: relative redirects dropped query strings). Diff only touches that theme. Run anti-thrash preflight and prefer targeted contextual re-review over another Full council. Default merge-blocker filing. Review only; no edits.

After the routing announce, continue with tools — read the diff and code-review skill, then produce the review. Do not stop at the announce.

Required: header includes `Pass: targeted contextual` and `Stayed targeted contextual`; reconstruct ledger; do not invent a sibling Action theme.

judge: The re-review stays targeted contextual after anti-thrash preflight, reconstructs prior themes, uses findings-first output without a Fix-loop theme table, and does not invent a sibling Action theme

scenarios.name: pr: anti-thrash signal collapse
scenarios.prompt:
Review the PR diff for agent-suites/fixtures/sample-app/src/redirect.ts (main...HEAD). Two adjacent blocker candidates exist in the same ownership/path family (query dropped vs absolute target accepted). Apply the thrash signal: stop filing separate Action symptoms and collapse into one theme with a same-invariant sweep. Default merge-blocker filing. Review only; no edits.

After the routing announce, continue with tools — read the diff and code-review skill, then produce the review. Do not stop at the announce.

judge: The review applies the thrash signal, collapses adjacent same-family blockers into one theme_id with an explicit same-invariant sweep, and does not file sibling Action blocks for each edge

scenarios.name: pr: bare re-review stays targeted
scenarios.prompt:
review vs main

Review only; no edits.

After the routing announce, continue with tools — read the diff and code-review skill (including output.md findings-first Continuity rules), then produce the Review · report. Do not stop at the announce.

judge: The re-review classifies closure-re-review via commit-stack archaeology, stays targeted contextual, uses findings-first output without a Fix-loop theme table, and does not invent a sibling Action theme

scenarios.name: pr: large branch narrow fix stays targeted
scenarios.prompt:
review vs main

Prior Action finding Theme: `query-preservation` is open; latest tip only touches redirect.ts. Re-review after that narrow fix. Default merge-blocker filing. Review only; no edits.

After the routing announce, continue with tools — read the diff and code-review skill (including output.md findings-first Continuity rules), then produce the Review · report. Do not stop at the announce.

judge: Despite a large whole-branch diff surface, the re-review stays targeted contextual for closure-re-review, records that size thresholds were not used to Full-promote, and keeps findings-first output without a Fix-loop theme table

scenarios.name: pr: premature closure reopens same theme
scenarios.prompt:
review vs main

Prior Action finding Theme: `query-preservation` was marked closed after fixing query-only (E1). Re-review after that fix. Default merge-blocker filing. Review only; no edits.

After the routing announce, continue with tools — read the diff and code-review skill (including output.md findings-first Continuity rules), then produce the Review · report. Do not stop at the announce.

Required: emit a full `Review · …` report (header, Findings, synthesis). Do not end the turn after Routing alone.

judge: The re-review detects premature closure, reopens the same theme_id query-preservation, includes a Continuity one-liner (not a Fix-loop theme table), and does not claim the exit gate passed

scenarios.name: pr: thrash reopen forces sweep before more Action

review vs main

After the routing announce, continue with tools — read the diff and code-review skill (including output.md findings-first Continuity rules), then produce the Review · report. Do not stop at the announce.

judge: The re-review applies thrash signal for a reopened theme, requires same-invariant sweep completion under one theme_id, includes a Continuity one-liner without a Fix-loop theme table, and does not file a second Action theme

scenarios.name: pr: green exit deletes leftover ledger
scenarios.prompt:
review vs main

Prior Action finding Theme: `query-preservation` is closed with complete variants; tip is the complete fix. Expect a green exit gate. Delete leftover `_agent/review/REVIEW_LEDGER.md` if present. Findings-first (no Continuity line, no theme table). Default merge-blocker filing. Review only; no edits.

After the routing announce, continue with tools — read the diff and code-review skill (including output.md findings-first Continuity rules), then produce the Review · report. Do not stop at the announce.

Required: emit a full `Review · …` report (header, Findings, synthesis). Do not end the turn after Routing alone.

judge: On a green exit gate the review deletes the leftover review ledger file if present, omits Continuity and theme-table sections, and stays targeted contextual
